summary: The diff implements all four requirements: admin registration flow (REQ-1), admin login page (REQ-2), AdminGuard route protection (REQ-3), and admin dashboard with logout (REQ-4). The implementation is largely complete, but there is one notable deviation in REQ-1: after successful registration, the spec requires redirect to /admin/login, but the code redirects to /admin/dashboard instead. Additionally, the registration page auto-sets an admin session after registration rather than requiring the user to log in separately.

missing_requirements.reason: AdminRegistrationPage.tsx calls setAdminSession(email) and then navigates to /admin/dashboard after successful registration, instead of redirecting to /admin/login as specified.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication step required before admin registration form submission
hallucinated_features.reason: The registration page requires the user to authenticate with Internet Identity via useInternetIdentity hook before submitting the registration form. This was not mentioned in the build request.

hallucinated_features.description: Auto-login after registration: setAdminSession is called immediately after successful registration and user is redirected to /admin/dashboard
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec says to redirect to /admin/login after registration implying the user must log in separately, but the code automatically creates a session and redirects to /admin/dashboard.

hallucinated_features.description: Data migration module added as a separate backend/migration.mo file
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request constraint requires all Motoko logic to remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor, but a separate migration.mo module was added.
